message:
test(fp-conformance): split CI supply-chain policy tests (br-frankenpandas-lxhr)

Continuation of cod-pandas's br-lxhr monolith-split work that was
left uncommitted when all COD agents hit the Codex usage limit.
Moves three CI-policy inline tests out of fp-conformance/src/lib.rs
into their own sub-module under crates/fp-conformance/src/tests/:

  ci_workflow_runs_supply_chain_security_scans
  dependabot_tracks_cargo_and_actions_weekly
  deny_toml_locks_license_and_source_policy

Same pattern as the earlier lxhr split commits (965885c /
93455dd / a23f7d3 / b0ea5cf / 1e942d7 / 24e2031 / 719f2c9 /
f2e4344). Net change in lib.rs: -69 lines. Net add in
tests/ci_supply_chain_policy.rs: +77 lines (includes the
repo_root() helper factored out of the inline mod).

Tests under rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenpandas_cc):
  cargo test -p fp-conformance --lib ci_supply_chain_policy
    → 3 passed / 0 failed
  cargo test -p fp-conformance --lib
    → 413 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored  (workspace stays green)

Picking up the lxhr slice cod was on because I'm now SOLO on
pandas until Apr 28 per orchestrator directive. Further slices
of the monolith split can be picked up from the same lxhr
umbrella on the next cc-pandas turn.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
